load_type.title: Load Type
load_type.description: Full load re-reads everything each run. Incremental load filters by a date field from a chosen starting point.

date_field.title: Date Field
date_field.description: Date field to filter on. Only some endpoints offer date filters.

date_from.title: Date From
date_from.description: Lower bound for the selected date field. Accepts 'last_run' (continue from last run), a relative value like 'yesterday' or '5 days ago', or an ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD).

columns.title: Columns to extract
columns.description: Optional; restrict the output table to these columns; leave empty to extract all columns; primary-key columns are always included.

probe.title: Probe
probe.description: Preview real sample data for this object before running a full extraction.

probe_limit.title: Sample size
probe_limit.description:
Preview real sample data for this object before committing to a full extraction. Set the sample size and click **Run Probe**.

With an object selected (this row), it returns up to this many real records (1–20), flattened exactly as the extraction writes them, plus the object's primary key, date fields and columns. Run it from the connection with no object selected to instead list every available object with its primary key, date fields and columns.

The result is a JSON object inside the result message (a fenced ```json block).

Use what it returns to set the row up:
- if the object has date fields → set **Load Type** to incremental and choose a **Date Field**
- pick the **Columns to extract** you want (the primary key is always kept)

Keep the sample size small; very large results may be truncated.
